name: Respond – Incident Response & Reporting
description: Processes and capabilities to respond to cybersecurity incidents effectively, minimize impact, communicate clearly and comply with regulatory reporting obligations.

maturity_scale.0: Not implemented – no defined response capability.
maturity_scale.1: Ad hoc – reactions are improvised and undocumented.
maturity_scale.2: Partially defined – some procedures exist but are incomplete or inconsistently applied.
maturity_scale.3: Defined and implemented – documented and communicated incident response plan followed by staff.
maturity_scale.4: Measured and monitored – response effectiveness is tested and improved.
maturity_scale.5: Optimized – proactive, well-coordinated response integrated with business continuity and external stakeholders.

questions.text: Is there a formally documented and approved incident response plan (IRP)?
questions.description: Assesses whether the organization has a written, approved plan describing detection, analysis, containment, eradication and recovery procedures.

questions.text: Are incident response roles, responsibilities and communication channels clearly defined?
questions.description: Evaluates whether staff know who to contact, escalation paths, and responsibilities during incidents.

questions.text: Are cybersecurity incidents recorded, classified and analyzed to identify root causes and lessons learned?
questions.description: Checks whether incidents are documented in a structured way, categorized and followed by post-incident analysis.

questions.text: Are incident response exercises or simulations conducted periodically?
questions.description: Assesses whether the organization tests its IRP through tabletop or technical simulations to evaluate readiness.

questions.text: Is there a defined process for timely notification to regulators, partners and affected parties as required (e.g., NIS2 reporting)?
questions.description: Evaluates compliance with external reporting obligations and coordination with stakeholders during incidents.

questions.text: Are incident response improvements tracked and implemented after reviews or exercises?
questions.description: Checks whether lessons learned and corrective actions from incidents or drills are systematically implemented and verified.

recommendations.title: Develop and approve a formal Incident Response Plan (IRP)
recommendations.description: The organization lacks a documented incident response plan, resulting in inconsistent reactions to security events.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Create a formal IRP defining detection, analysis, containment, eradication and recovery phases. Include communication, reporting and escalation steps. Obtain management approval and review at least annually.

recommendations.title: Define incident response roles and communication structure
recommendations.description: Responsibilities and communication channels during incidents are unclear, causing delays and confusion.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Assign specific roles (Incident Manager, Communications Lead, Technical Lead, Legal/Compliance). Document escalation matrix and contact lists. Train staff on when and how to report incidents.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RS.CO (Incident Response Reporting and Communication)

recommendations.title: Record and analyze incidents for root causes and improvements
recommendations.description: Incidents are handled but not analyzed systematically, missing opportunities to prevent recurrence.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Maintain an incident register recording type, impact, root cause and corrective actions. Conduct post-incident reviews and feed findings into security improvements and awareness programs.

recommendations.title: Conduct periodic incident response exercises
recommendations.description: Incident response capability is untested, leading to uncertainty during real incidents.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Plan and conduct at least annual IR exercises (tabletop or technical). Evaluate timing, decision-making and communication effectiveness. Document lessons learned and integrate into IRP updates.

recommendations.title: Ensure compliance with regulatory and partner reporting requirements
recommendations.description: Regulatory or partner notification obligations are unclear or not met within required timelines.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Define criteria and time limits for notifying authorities and partners (e.g., NIS2 within 24 h initial, 72 h detailed). Prepare templates for rapid communication and maintain contact lists of relevant stakeholders.
recommendations.nist_ref: NIST CSF 2.0 – RS.CO (Incident Response Reporting and Communication)

recommendations.title: Track and implement corrective actions from incidents and exercises
recommendations.description: Lessons learned are identified but not acted upon, reducing continuous improvement of the response process.
recommendations.improvement_tips: Create a tracking log for all post-incident and exercise recommendations. Assign owners, deadlines and verify completion. Review implementation status in management reviews.
